1→"""Bundled skill capabilities — cached factory. 2→ 3→All simple skill capabilities (those that only need a name and description) 4→are registered here. The dict is created lazily via a cached function to 5→avoid module-import-time allocation. 6→ 7→Skills with custom install/uninstall logic (like learned-docs) are NOT here; 8→they have their own capability classes. 9→""" 10→ 11→from functools import cache 12→ 13→from erk.core.capabilities.codex_portable import codex_portable_skills 14→from erk.core.capabilities.skill_capability import SkillCapability 15→from erk_shared.context.types import AgentBackend 16→ 17→_UNBUNDLED_SKILLS: frozenset[str] = frozenset( 18→ { 19→ "ci-iteration", 20→ "cli-skill-creator", 21→ "cmux", 22→ "command-creator", 23→ "learned-docs", # has its own capability class (LearnedDocsCapability) 24→ "refac-cli-push-down", 25→ "refac-mock-to-fake", 26→ "refac-module-to-subpackage", 27→ "rename-swarm", 28→ "session-inspector", 29→ "erk-skill-onboarding", 30→ "skill-creator",

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:
## Summary of Findings
### ArtifactSyncConfig Occurrences
I found **14 direct instantiations** of `ArtifactSyncConfig(` across the codebase:
#### 1. Production Code (2 occurrences)
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/artifacts/sync.py` (lines 62-67)** - Function: `create_artifact_sync_config()` - Purpose: Factory function for production use - Parameters used: - `package`: `ErkPackageInfo.from_project_dir(project_dir)` - `installed_capabilities`: `load_installed_capabilities(project_dir)` - `sync_capabilities`: `True` - `backend`: passed as parameter
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/artifact/sync_cmd.py` (lines 47-50)** - Context: CLI command implementation - Parameters used: - `package`: `ctx.obj.package_info` - `installed_capabilities`: `load_installed_capabilities(project_dir)` - `sync_capabilities`: `not dry_run`
#### 2. Test Code (12 occurrences)
All remaining instances are in test files:
- **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/artifacts/test_sync.py`** (10 occurrences at lines: 39, 61, 95, 128, 272, 461, 509, 600, 637, 674) - **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/artifacts/test_orphan_cleanup.py`** (1 occurrence at line 23)
All test instances follow a consistent pattern with `ErkPackageInfo` and `sync_capabilities=False` to avoid capability install issues during testing.
---
### ArtifactSyncConfig Class Definition
**Location: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/artifacts/sync.py` (lines 46-54)**
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class ArtifactSyncConfig: """Configuration for artifact sync - enables testing without mocks."""
package: ErkPackageInfo installed_capabilities: frozenset[str] sync_capabilities: bool # False in tests to avoid capability install overwriting test fixtures backend: AgentBackend ```
**Required Parameters:** - `package`: ErkPackageInfo object (required, always provided) - `installed_capabilities`: frozenset[str] (required, always provided) - `sync_capabilities`: bool (required, always provided - True in production, False in tests) - `backend`: AgentBackend (required, only in production code)
---
### Bundled Skills Definition
**Location: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/capabilities/skills/bundled.py` (lines 45-60)**
The `bundled_skills()` function returns a cached dictionary with 11 bundled skills:
```python @cache def bundled_skills() -> dict[str, str]: """Return the bundled skills dict. Cached to avoid re-creation.""" return { "dignified-python": "Python coding standards (LBYL, modern types, ABCs)", "fake-driven-testing": "5-layer test architecture with fakes", "erk-diff-analysis": "Code diff analysis for commit messages", "erk-exec": "Erk exec subcommand reference", "erk-planning": "Plan management", "objective": "Objective tracking and management", "gh": "GitHub CLI integration", "gt": "Graphite stacked PR management", "dignified-code-simplifier": "Code simplification review", "pr-operations": "Pull request operations", "pr-feedback-classifier": "PR feedback classification", } ```
#### Supporting Functions
**`unbundled_skills()`** (line 63-65): Returns a frozenset of 12 skills intentionally NOT bundled with erk (erk-repo-only): - ci-iteration, cli-skill-creator, cmux, command-creator, learned-docs, refac-cli-push-down, refac-mock-to-fake, refac-module-to-subpackage, rename-swarm, session-inspector, erk-skill-onboarding, skill-creator
**`is_required_bundled_skill()`** (line 68-70): Returns True if a skill is in the required set: - erk-diff-analysis, erk-exec, objective, pr-operations, pr-feedback-classifier
#### Usage Locations
`bundled_skills()` is called in: - **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/capabilities/skills/bundled.py`** (line 103): In `create_bundled_skill_capabilities()` factory - **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/core/capabilities/test_base.py`** (line 65): Capability registry test - **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/core/capabilities/test_skills.py`** (line 62): Skill registration test - **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/core/capabilities/test_registry.py`** (line 94): Registry scope test - **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/artifacts/test_codex_compatibility.py`** (lines 180, 273): Codex compatibility validation
